compiled_state.description:
# Plan-review threshold + posture recalibration (approved pre-redesign changes)

## Why
Adjudication of would-block/near-block plan-review findings showed the gate UNDER-blocks: 15/20 findings
in the 0.60-0.69 band on blocking criteria were block-worthy with ZERO false-positives, and four
currently-advisory criteria (G1G2, T1, T8, E4) scored 75-100% block-worthy at their would-block slice.
The 0.70 bar strands real blocks and the blocking set omits load-bearing criteria. (E1 and T3 also scored
~75% but are NOT promoted here — no threshold cleanly separates their borderline findings; deferred.)

## What
Apply the approved per-criterion thresholds/postures in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json`:
- Lower `block_threshold` 0.70 -> 0.60 for exactly: COH, E2, F1, G5, G6, T5e.
- Keep T4 @ 0.70 (its band findings were fail-safe migrations).
- Promote to `default_posture: blocking`: G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75.
- E1, T3, A1 remain advisory. No other criterion changes.

## Scope
- Edit only the routing JSON + the plan-review unit tests asserting per-criterion `block_threshold` /
  `default_posture`. No engine or aggregation changes here (the impact redesign is a separate child).
- This is a deliberate BREAKING behavioral change: plans that passed before may now BLOCK. Rollout is
  coupled to the permissive-rollout child (ship the wider set first, tune via the calibration harness);
  rollback is reverting the JSON (single-commit, trivially reversible).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `criteria_routing.json` matches the table above EXACTLY (per-criterion `block_threshold` +
      `default_posture`); a test asserts each of the SIX lowered (COH/E2/F1/G5/G6/T5e @ 0.60) + the FOUR
      promoted (G1G2/T1/T8 blocking @ 0.70, E4 blocking @ 0.75) + T4 UNCHANGED (@ 0.70, blocking).
- [ ] Plan-review unit tests updated and green; `make lint` passes. Proving command:
      `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py -v`.
- [ ] DETERMINISTIC simulation (no live corpus): using the packaged routing + the shared
      `threshold_for` / `pass3_decide`, a test asserts that for EACH of the six lowered criteria a synthetic
      finding at priority 0.65 now BLOCKS (>= 0.60) whereas at the OLD 0.70 it would NOT have; and that for
      EACH of the four promoted criteria the posture is now `blocking` and a finding blocks only at/above its
      NEW threshold (0.70 or 0.75). (Live-corpus block-rate delta on the accumulated sidecar corpus is
      measured by the calibration child raptorial-galloping-dragon, NOT gated here.)
- [ ] No criterion OUTSIDE the approved set {COH,E2,F1,G5,G6,T5e,T4,G1G2,T1,T8,E4} changes posture or
      threshold. A test pins the COMPLETE expected routing as an INLINE fixture inside
      `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` — a dict mapping EVERY criterion id to its expected
      `(block_threshold, default_posture)` — and asserts the packaged `criteria_routing.json` matches it
      exactly. Any unintended change to ANY criterion (approved or not) fails the assertion; the expectation
      lives in the test (versioned with it), so there is NO separate baseline snapshot file to drift.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review threshold + posture recalibration (approved pre-redesign changes)
